volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Antigone (Section 1.1265 - Section 1347)

sections.label: Section 1.1265
sections.original: ὤμοι ἐμῶν ἄνολβα βουλευμάτων .
sections.translation: What misery arises from my reasonings ! Haemon , you have died after a young life , youngest and last of my sons ! O God ! You have departed not by your foolishness , but by my own !

sections.label: Section 2.1270
sections.original: οἴμʼ ὡς ἔοικας ὀψὲ τὴν δίκην ἰδεῖν .
sections.translation: Ah , how late you seem to see the right !

sections.label: Section 2.1271
sections.original: οἴμοι ,
sections.translation: God , I have mastered the bitter lesson ! But then , then , I think , some god struck me on my head with a crushing weight , and drove me into savage paths ,

sections.label: Section 2.1275
sections.original: οἴμοι , λακπάτητον ἀντρέπων χαράν .
sections.translation: — ah !— and overthrew my joy to be trampled on ! Ah , the labors men must toil through !

sections.label: Section 2.1278
sections.original: ὦ δέσποθʼ , ὡς ἔχων τε καὶ κεκτημένος ,
sections.translation: My master , you have come , I think , like one whose hands are not empty , but who has a ready store : first , you carry that burden visible in your arms ;

sections.label: Section 2.1280
sections.original: ἔοικας ἥκειν καὶ τάχʼ ὄψεσθαι κακά .
sections.translation: second , you will soon look upon further sufferings inside your house .

sections.label: Section 2.1281
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν αὖ κάκιον ἐκ κακῶν ἔτι ;
sections.translation: What worse suffering is still to follow upon these sufferings ?

sections.label: Section 2.1282
sections.original: γυνὴ τέθνηκε , τοῦδε παμμήτωρ νεκροῦ ,
sections.translation: Your wife is dead , true mother of that corpse , poor lady , by wounds newly cut .

sections.label: Section 1.1284
sections.original: ἰώ .
sections.translation: O harbor of Hades , hard to purify !

sections.label: Section 1.1285
sections.original: τί μʼ ἄρα τί μʼ ὀλέκεις ;
sections.translation: Why , why do you ruin me ? Herald of evil , of grief , what word do you say ? Ah , you have done in a dead man anew ! What are you saying , boy ? What is this you report to me

sections.label: Section 1.1290
sections.original: αἰαῖ αἰαῖ ,
sections.translation: God no !— what new slaughter , my wife ’ s doom , is heaped upon this ruin ?

sections.label: Section 1.1293
sections.original: ὁρᾶν πάρεστιν · οὐ γὰρ ἐν μυχοῖς ἔτι .
sections.translation: The sight is at hand . It is no longer hidden inside .

sections.label: Section 2.1294
sections.original: οἴμοι ,
sections.translation: Ah , misery !

sections.label: Section 2.1295
sections.original: κακὸν τόδʼ ἄλλο δεύτερον βλέπω τάλας .
sections.translation: There I see a new , a second evil ! What destiny , ah , what , can still await me ? I have just now taken my son in my arms , and now I see another corpse before me !

sections.label: Section 2.1300
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ μᾶτερ ἀθλία , φεῦ τέκνον .
sections.translation: Oh , tormented mother ! Oh , my son !

sections.label: Section 2.1301
sections.original: ἡ δʼ ὀξυθήκτῳ βωμία περὶ ξίφει
sections.translation: By the altar , with a sharp-whetted sword , she struck until her eyes went slack and dark . Before that she bewailed the noble fate of Megareus who died earlier , and then the fate of this boy , and also , with her last breath ,

sections.label: Section 2.1305
sections.original: πράξεις ἐφυμνήσασα τῷ παιδοκτόνῳ .
sections.translation: she called down evil fortune upon you , the slayer of her sons .

sections.label: Section 3.1306
sections.original: αἰαῖ αἰαῖ ,
sections.translation: Ah , no ! I tremble with fear . Why does no one strike me full on my chest with a two-edged sword ?

sections.label: Section 3.1310
sections.original: δείλαιος ἐγώ , αἰαῖ ,
sections.translation: I am miserable — ah — and bathed in miserable anguish !

sections.label: Section 3.1312
sections.original: ὡς αἰτίαν γε τῶνδε κἀκείνων ἔχων
sections.translation: Yes , because you were accused of responsibility for both this son ’ s death , and the other ’ s , by her whose corpse you see .

sections.label: Section 3.1314
sections.original: ποίῳ δὲ κἀπελύσατʼ ἐν φοναῖς τρόπῳ ;
sections.translation: What was the manner of the violent deed by which she departed ?

sections.label: Section 3.1315
sections.original: παίσασ ' ὑφʼ ἧπαρ αὐτόχειρ αὑτήν , ὅπως
sections.translation: Her own hand struck her to the heart upon learning her son ’ s sharply-lamented fate .

sections.label: Section 4.1317
sections.original: ὤμοι μοι , τάδʼ οὐκ ἐπʼ ἄλλον βροτῶν
sections.translation: Ah this guilt can never be fastened onto any other mortal so as to remove my own ! It was I , yes , I , who killed you , I the wretch .

sections.label: Section 4.1320
sections.original: ἐγώ , φάμʼ ἔτυμον . ἰὼ πρόσπολοι ,
sections.translation: I admit the truth . Lead me away , my servants , lead me from here with all haste , who am no more than a dead man !

sections.label: Section 4.1325
sections.original: τὸν οὐκ ὄντα μᾶλλον ἢ μηδένα .
sections.translation: The course you recommend is to your gain , if there can be gain amidst evil . What is briefest is best , when trouble lies at your feet .

sections.label: Section 3.1328
sections.original: ἴτω ἴτω ,
sections.translation: Let it come , let it appear , that fairest of fates for me , that brings my final day ,

sections.label: Section 3.1330
sections.original: ἐμοὶ τερμίαν ἄγων ἁμέραν
sections.translation: the fate supreme ! Oh , let it come , so that I may never see tomorrow ’ s light !

sections.label: Section 3.1334
sections.original: μέλλοντα ταῦτα . τῶν προκειμένων τι χρὴ
sections.translation: These things are in the future . We must see to present affairs .

sections.label: Section 3.1335
sections.original: πράσσειν . μέλει γὰρ τῶνδʼ ὅτοισι χρὴ μέλειν .
sections.translation: Fulfillment of these things rests in the hands where it should rest .

sections.label: Section 3.1336
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὧν ἐρῶ , τοιαῦτα συγκατηυξάμην .
sections.translation: All that I crave was summed in that prayer .

sections.label: Section 3.1337
sections.original: μή νυν προσεύχου μηδέν · ὡς πεπρωμένης
sections.translation: Then pray no more ; for mortals have no release from destined misfortune .

sections.label: Section 4.1339
sections.original: ἄγοιτʼ ἂν μάταιον ἄνδρʼ ἐκποδών ,
sections.translation: Lead me away , I beg you , a rash , useless man .

sections.label: Section 4.1340
sections.original: ὅς , ὦ παῖ , σέ τʼ οὐχ ἑκὼν κάκτανον
sections.translation: I have murdered you , son , unwittingly , and you , too , my wife — the misery ! I do not know which way I should look , or where I should seek support . All is

sections.label: Section 4.1345
sections.original: λέχρια τἀν χεροῖν , τὰ δʼ ἐπὶ κρατί μοι
sections.translation: amiss that is in my hands , and , again , a crushing fate has leapt upon my head .

sections.label: Section 1347
sections.original: πολλῷ τὸ φρονεῖν εὐδαιμονίας
sections.translation: Wisdom is provided as the chief part of happiness , and our dealings with the gods must be in no way unholy . The great words of arrogant men have to make repayment with great blows , and in old age teach wisdom .
